description: HP 8720C microwave vector network analyzer, 50 MHz to 20 GHz, HP-IB. Uses the HP 8753/8720-series mnemonic command language (not pure SCPI). Commands documented in this reference are shared across the HP 8719C, 8720C, and 8722A/C analyzers and are largely compatible with the HP 8753.

identity.description: Outputs the identification string "HEWLETT PACKARD, 87aam,0,X.XX" where aam is the rest of the model number and X.XX is the firmware revision. Also accessible via IDN? and *IDN?.

commands.name: address_controller
commands.description: Sets the HP-IB address the analyzer uses to communicate with an external controller (also accepts PCB). Must match the system controller interface for pass-control.

commands.name: address_disk
commands.description: Sets the HP-IB address the analyzer uses to communicate with an external disk drive.

commands.name: address_plotter
commands.description: Sets the HP-IB address the analyzer uses to communicate with a plotter.

commands.name: address_power_meter
commands.description: Sets the HP-IB address the analyzer uses to communicate with a compatible power meter.

commands.name: address_printer
commands.description: Sets the HP-IB address the analyzer uses to communicate with the printer.

commands.name: alc
commands.description: Controls the automatic leveling control (ALC) of the test port power.

commands.name: debug_mode
commands.description: Turns the HP-IB command debug mode on or off; when on, commands scroll across the top of the display.

commands.name: talker_listener
commands.description: Puts the analyzer in talker/listener HP-IB mode.

commands.name: use_pass_control
commands.description: Puts the analyzer in use-pass-control HP-IB mode.

commands.name: disk_unit_number
commands.description: Specifies the disk unit number in a multiple-drive disk for store/load (e.g. 0 = left drive, 1 = right drive).

commands.name: disk_volume_number
commands.description: Specifies the volume number in a disk drive that allows multiple volumes for store/load.

commands.name: noop
commands.description: The "no operation" command.

commands.name: execute_test
commands.description: Executes the selected service test.

commands.name: firmware_revision
commands.description: Displays the firmware revision in the active entry area.

commands.name: instrument_mode_na
commands.description: Selects instrument mode as a network analyzer.

commands.name: instrument_mode_tuned_receiver
commands.description: Selects instrument mode as a tuned receiver.

commands.name: test_set_id
commands.description: Outputs the test set identifier: 1 for an S-parameter test set, 0 for none.

commands.name: continuous_switching
commands.description: Enables or disables continuous switching of the test set transfer switch.

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Outputs the identification string, "HEWLETT PACKARD, 87aam,0,X.XX", where aam is the rest of the model number and X.XX is the firmware revision. Equivalent to IDN? and *IDN?.

commands.name: center
commands.description: Sets the center stimulus value (units depend on sweep domain: frequency, time, or power).

commands.name: span
commands.description: Sets the stimulus span. If a list-frequency segment is being edited, sets the segment span.

commands.name: start
commands.description: Defines the start stimulus value. If a list-frequency segment is being edited, sets the segment start.

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Defines the stop stimulus value. If a list-frequency segment is being edited, sets the segment stop.

commands.name: cw_frequency
commands.description: Sets the frequency for CW frequency mode and power sweep. When editing a list segment, sets the segment center frequency with span 0 Hz.

commands.name: number_of_points
commands.description: Sets the number of data points per sweep.

commands.name: power_level
commands.description: Sets the source output level within the current power range (see PRAN).

commands.name: power_range
commands.description: Selects a specific power range (01-12) for test port power. See Specifications for the level selectable with POWE.

commands.name: sweep_time
commands.description: Sets the sweep time manually.

commands.name: sweep_time_auto
commands.description: Selects automatic sweep time (fastest possible for the given frequency range, points, and IF bandwidth).

commands.name: step_size
commands.description: When editing a list-frequency segment, sets the step size.

commands.name: linear_frequency
commands.description: Activates a linear frequency sweep.

commands.name: log_frequency
commands.description: Activates a log frequency sweep.

commands.name: list_frequency
commands.description: Activates the frequency list sweep mode.

commands.name: power_sweep
commands.description: Activates power sweep mode.

commands.name: cw_time
commands.description: Selects the CW time sweep type.

commands.name: step_sweep
commands.description: When on, forces a stepped sweep on every point (rather than a swept sweep).

commands.name: all_segments_sweep
commands.description: Selects measurement of all list-frequency segments in LIST FREQ mode.

commands.name: single_segment_sweep
commands.description: Selects measurement of a specific list-frequency segment number in LIST FREQ mode.

commands.name: coupled_channels
commands.description: Sets channel coupling of stimulus values on or off.

commands.name: continuous
commands.description: Continuous trigger.

commands.name: free_run
commands.description: Continuous trigger (free run).

commands.name: single
commands.description: Makes a single measurement sweep, then enters hold mode.

commands.name: hold
commands.description: Sets the trigger mode to hold the current measurement.

commands.name: number_of_groups
commands.description: Triggers a user-specified number of sweeps, then returns to hold mode.

commands.name: ext_trigger_off
commands.description: Internal trigger mode on, external trigger off.

commands.name: ext_trigger_on_sweep
commands.description: Enables external measurement trigger on sweep; one sweep per trigger.

commands.name: ext_trigger_on_point
commands.description: Enables external measurement trigger on point; one point per trigger.

commands.name: manual_trigger_point
commands.description: Triggers a measurement at one point (manual trigger on point).

commands.name: hpib_trigger
commands.description: HP-IB trigger; puts the analyzer into hold.

commands.name: measure_restart
commands.description: Aborts the sweep in progress and restarts the measurement.

commands.name: wait_sweep
commands.description: Waits for a clean (complete) sweep.

commands.name: meas_s11
commands.description: Selects measurement of S11 (reflection, forward).

commands.name: meas_s12
commands.description: Selects measurement of S12 (transmission, reverse).

commands.name: meas_s21
commands.description: Selects measurement of S21 (transmission, forward).

commands.name: meas_s22
commands.description: Selects measurement of S22 (reflection, reverse).

commands.name: meas_input_a
commands.description: Measures the absolute power amplitude at input A.

commands.name: meas_input_b
commands.description: Measures the absolute power amplitude at input B.

commands.name: meas_input_r
commands.description: Measures the absolute power amplitude at input R.

commands.name: aux_input
commands.description: Measures and displays the data at the auxiliary input.

commands.name: channel_1
commands.description: Selects channel 1 as the active measurement channel.

commands.name: channel_2
commands.description: Selects channel 2 as the active measurement channel.

commands.name: alternate_ab
commands.description: Alternates measurement of the A and B samplers between sweeps when both are required.

commands.name: chop_ab
commands.description: Simultaneously measures both A and B samplers in a single sweep when both are required.

commands.name: convert_off
commands.description: Turns off all parameter conversion operations.

commands.name: convert_inverse_s
commands.description: Converts data into inverse S-parameter (1/S) values.

commands.name: convert_y_refl
commands.description: Converts reflection data to equivalent admittance (Y) values.

commands.name: convert_y_trans
commands.description: Converts transmission data to equivalent admittance (Y) values.

commands.name: convert_z_refl
commands.description: Converts reflection data to equivalent impedance (Z) values.

commands.name: convert_z_trans
commands.description: Converts transmission data to equivalent impedance (Z) values.

commands.name: format_log_mag
commands.description: Displays log magnitude format.

commands.name: format_lin_mag
commands.description: Displays linear magnitude format.

commands.name: format_phase
commands.description: Displays Cartesian phase format (degrees).

commands.name: format_delay
commands.description: Selects the group delay format for the current measurement.

commands.name: format_smith
commands.description: Displays a Smith chart format.

commands.name: format_polar
commands.description: Displays polar format.

commands.name: format_real
commands.description: Displays the real part of the data in Cartesian format.

commands.name: format_swr
commands.description: Selects SWR display for the active channel.

commands.name: scale_per_div
commands.description: Sets the response value scale per division of the graticule.

commands.name: auto_scale
commands.description: Automatically selects the scale/div to fit the trace to the display.

commands.name: reference_position
commands.description: Sets the position of the reference line on the graticule (Cartesian formats).

commands.name: reference_value
commands.description: Changes the value of the reference line, moving the trace correspondingly.

commands.name: electrical_delay
commands.description: Sets the electrical delay.

commands.name: phase_offset
commands.description: Adds or subtracts a phase offset.

commands.name: coaxial_delay
commands.description: Selects electrical delay calculation based on a coaxial transmission line.

commands.name: waveguide_delay
commands.description: Selects electrical delay calculation based on a waveguide transmission line; value is the waveguide cutoff frequency.

commands.name: velocity_factor
commands.description: Sets the velocity factor used to calculate equivalent electrical length.

commands.name: averaging
commands.description: Sets the averaging function on or off for the active channel.

commands.name: averaging_factor
commands.description: Sets the averaging factor.

commands.name: averaging_restart
commands.description: Resets and restarts averaging.

commands.name: if_bandwidth
commands.description: Sets the IF bandwidth value.

commands.name: smoothing
commands.description: Sets the smoothing function on or off.

commands.name: smoothing_aperture
commands.description: Sets the smoothing aperture as a percent of the span.

commands.name: data_to_memory
commands.description: Stores the active channel data to trace memory.

commands.name: display_data
commands.description: Displays a trace of the measured data.

commands.name: display_memory
commands.description: Displays the trace of the memory data.

commands.name: display_data_and_memory
commands.description: Displays both the measured data and memory data traces.

commands.name: display_data_div_memory
commands.description: Displays the trace of measured data divided by memory data (data/mem). Also DIVI.

commands.name: display_data_minus_memory
commands.description: Displays the trace of measured data minus memory data (data-mem). Also MINU.

commands.name: dual_channel
commands.description: Selects dual channel display on or off.

commands.name: split_display
commands.description: Selects split display: full-screen single graticule (OFF) or two half-screen graticules (ON).

commands.name: intensity
commands.description: Sets the display intensity as a percent of the brightest setting.

commands.name: background_intensity
commands.description: Sets the background intensity as a percent of the white level.

commands.name: frequency_blank
commands.description: Erases the frequency annotation on the display (preset turns it on).

commands.name: beep_done
commands.description: Sets the operation-complete beeper on or off.

commands.name: beep_warn
commands.description: Sets the warning beeper on or off.

commands.name: beep_fail
commands.description: Sets the limit-fail beeper on or off.

commands.name: title
commands.description: Sends a string to the title area of the display.

commands.name: active_entry_off
commands.description: Turns off the active entry area.

commands.name: entry_up
commands.description: Increments the value in the active entry area (up-arrow key).

commands.name: entry_down
commands.description: Decrements the value in the active entry area (down-arrow key).

commands.name: key_code
commands.description: Inputs the key code for a front-panel hardkey or softkey (equivalent to pressing the key). See key code table.

commands.name: color_select
commands.description: Specifies the display element whose color is to be changed (channel data/memory/limit, graticule, text, warning); used with CBRI/COLOR/TINT.

commands.name: color_saturation
commands.description: Specifies the saturation percent of the selected display element.

commands.name: color_brightness
commands.description: Sets the color brightness (in percent) of the selected display element.

commands.name: color_tint
commands.description: Adjusts the hue of the selected display element.

commands.name: default_colors
commands.description: Returns all traces, lines, and text to the default colors.

commands.name: reset_color
commands.description: Resets the modified colors to the default colors.

commands.name: recall_colors
commands.description: Recalls the previously saved color set.

commands.name: save_colors
commands.description: Saves the modified color set.

commands.name: marker
commands.description: Selects the active marker and moves it to the specified stimulus value (markers 1-5).

commands.name: markers_all_off
commands.description: Turns off all markers and the delta reference marker.

commands.name: marker_continuous
commands.description: Interpolates between measured points so markers can be placed anywhere on the trace.

commands.name: marker_discrete
commands.description: Places markers only on measured trace points.

commands.name: marker_coupled
commands.description: Couples the marker stimulus values for the two display channels.

commands.name: marker_uncoupled
commands.description: Allows marker stimulus values to be controlled independently per channel.

commands.name: delta_marker_off
commands.description: Sets the delta marker mode off.

commands.name: delta_reference_marker
commands.description: Sets the indicated marker (1-5) as the delta reference.

commands.name: delta_reference_fixed_marker
commands.description: Sets the user-specified fixed marker as the delta reference.

commands.name: marker_zero
commands.description: Puts a fixed reference marker at the active marker position and zeroes its stimulus and response.

commands.name: fixed_marker_stimulus
commands.description: Sets the fixed marker stimulus value offset.

commands.name: fixed_marker_value
commands.description: Sets the fixed marker position value offset.

commands.name: fixed_marker_aux_value
commands.description: Sets the fixed marker auxiliary value offset.

commands.name: marker_to_center
commands.description: Changes the stimulus center value to the active marker value.

commands.name: marker_to_span
commands.description: Changes the stimulus start/stop to the active marker and the delta reference marker.

commands.name: marker_to_start_stop
commands.description: Changes the stimulus start (MARKSTAR) or stop (MARKSTOP) value to the active marker value.

commands.name: marker_to_reference
commands.description: Changes the reference value to the active marker's response value without changing reference position.

commands.name: marker_to_delay
commands.description: Sets electrical length so group delay is zero at the marker stimulus.

commands.name: marker_to_stimulus
commands.description: While editing a limit segment, sets the stimulus value to the active marker value.

commands.name: marker_to_middle
commands.description: Sets the middle value for the delta limit using the active marker value.

commands.name: marker_to_bucket
commands.description: Moves the active marker to the specified data point (bucket) number.

commands.name: display_markers
commands.description: Enables or disables display of all individually turned-on markers below the active marker area.

commands.name: search_max
commands.description: Moves the active marker to the maximum point on the trace. Also MARKMAXI.

commands.name: search_min
commands.description: Moves the active marker to the minimum point on the trace. Also MARKMINI.

commands.name: search_left
commands.description: Searches the trace left of the marker for the next occurrence of the target value.

commands.name: search_off
commands.description: Turns off the search function.

commands.name: search_target
commands.description: Places the active marker at a specified target value on the trace.

commands.name: search_tracking
commands.description: Tracks the search at the specified target value with each new sweep.

commands.name: bandwidth_search
commands.description: Turns the bandwidth search feature on or off.

commands.name: bandwidth_width_value
commands.description: Sets the amplitude value defining the start/stop points of a bandwidth search.

commands.name: marker_statistics
commands.description: Calculates and displays (ON) the mean, standard deviation, and peak-to-peak values, or hides them (OFF).

commands.name: polar_marker_lin
commands.description: Displays the linear magnitude and phase of the active polar marker.

commands.name: polar_marker_log
commands.description: Displays the log magnitude and phase of the active polar marker.

commands.name: polar_marker_real_imag
commands.description: Displays a real/imaginary pair of the active polar marker.

commands.name: smith_marker_lin
commands.description: Displays the linear magnitude and phase of the active marker on a Smith chart.

commands.name: smith_marker_log
commands.description: Displays the log magnitude and phase of the active marker on a Smith chart.

commands.name: smith_marker_real_imag
commands.description: Displays the active marker as a real/imaginary pair on a Smith chart.

commands.name: smith_marker_r_jx
commands.description: Displays the complex impedance (R+jX) of the active marker on a Smith chart.

commands.name: smith_marker_g_jb
commands.description: Displays the complex admittance (G+jB) of the active marker on a Smith chart.

commands.name: correction
commands.description: Sets the error-correction function on or off.

commands.name: cal_menu
commands.description: Prepares for calibration (HP 8510 compatibility).

commands.name: cal_response
commands.description: Selects the response measurement calibration.

commands.name: cal_response_isolation
commands.description: Selects the response and isolation measurement calibration.

commands.name: cal_s11_1port
commands.description: Selects the 1-port measurement calibration at port 1 (S11).

commands.name: cal_s22_1port
commands.description: Selects the 1-port measurement calibration at port 2 (S22).

commands.name: cal_full_2port
commands.description: Selects the full 2-port measurement calibration.

commands.name: cal_trl_2port
commands.description: Selects the TRL*/LRM* 2-port measurement calibration.

commands.name: cal_kit_7mm
commands.description: Selects the 7 mm calibration kit.

commands.name: cal_kit_35mm
commands.description: Selects the 3.5 mm calibration kit.

commands.name: cal_kit_n50
commands.description: Selects the 50 ohm type-N calibration kit.

commands.name: cal_kit_trl
commands.description: Selects the TRL* calibration kit.

commands.name: cal_kit_user
commands.description: Selects the user-defined calibration kit.

commands.name: save_user_kit
commands.description: Stores the currently active calibration kit into user-kit memory.

commands.name: resume_cal
commands.description: Resumes the last measurement calibration sequence.

commands.name: response_done
commands.description: Completes the response calibration.

commands.name: response_isolation_done
commands.description: Completes the response and isolation calibration.

commands.name: rai_isolation
commands.description: Selects the isolation class for the response and isolation calibration.

commands.name: rai_response
commands.description: Selects the response class for the response and isolation calibration.

commands.name: isolation
commands.description: Begins the isolation part of the 2-port calibration.

commands.name: isolation_done
commands.description: Completes the isolation part of the 2-port calibration.

commands.name: omit_isolation
commands.description: Omits the isolation correction of a 2-port calibration.

commands.name: reflection
commands.description: Begins the reflection part of the full 2-port calibration.

commands.name: reflection_done
commands.description: Completes the reflection part of the full 2-port calibration.

commands.name: transmission
commands.description: Begins the transmission part of the full 2-port calibration.

commands.name: transmission_done
commands.description: Completes the transmission part of the full 2-port calibration.

commands.name: forward_isolation
commands.description: Selects forward isolation class for the calibration.

commands.name: forward_match
commands.description: Selects forward match class for the calibration.

commands.name: forward_transmission
commands.description: Selects forward transmission class for the calibration.

commands.name: reverse_isolation
commands.description: Selects reverse isolation class for the calibration.

commands.name: reverse_match
commands.description: Selects reverse match class for the calibration.

commands.name: reverse_transmission
commands.description: Selects reverse transmission class for the calibration.

commands.name: save_1port
commands.description: Computes and saves the 1-port calibration results.

commands.name: save_2port
commands.description: Computes and saves the full 2-port calibration results.

commands.name: save_trl
commands.description: Computes and saves the TRL*/LRM* 2-port calibration results.

commands.name: recompute_cal
commands.description: Re-draws a trace using the current error coefficient arrays.

commands.name: receiver_cal
commands.description: Begins the receiver calibration.

commands.name: take_receiver_cal_sweep
commands.description: Takes a receiver calibration sweep.

commands.name: freq_subset_cal
commands.description: Selects frequency-subset calibration on or off.

commands.name: set_z0
commands.description: Sets the characteristic impedance used in Smith-chart and conversion calculations.

commands.name: port_extensions
commands.description: Sets the reference plane (port) extension mode on or off.

commands.name: port_extension_1
commands.description: Sets the reference-plane extension for measurement of S11, S21, and S12.

commands.name: port_extension_2
commands.description: Sets the reference-plane extension for measurement of S22, S12, and S21.

commands.name: cal_power
commands.description: Sets the power level to be controlled by a power meter calibration.

commands.name: power_meter_cal
commands.description: Turns power meter calibration on or off.

commands.name: begin_power_meter_cal
commands.description: Begins the power meter calibration.

commands.name: power_meter_readings
commands.description: Sets the number of power meter readings per point during a power meter calibration.

commands.name: cal_z_line
commands.description: When defining a TRL-type cal kit, sets the LINE standard as the impedance reference.

commands.name: cal_z_system
commands.description: When defining a TRL-type cal kit, sets the SYSTEM Z0 value as the impedance reference.

commands.name: set_reference_reflect
commands.description: When defining a TRL-type cal kit, sets the REFLECT class to establish the measurement reference plane.

commands.name: set_reference_thru
commands.description: When defining a TRL-type cal kit, sets the THRU class to establish the measurement reference plane.

commands.name: trl_line_match_1
commands.description: Measures the port 1 LINE/MATCH class of the TRL*/LRM* 2-port calibration.

commands.name: trl_line_match_2
commands.description: Measures the port 2 LINE/MATCH class of the TRL*/LRM* 2-port calibration.

commands.name: trl_reflect_1
commands.description: Measures the S11 REFLECT class of the TRL*/LRM* 2-port calibration.

commands.name: trl_reflect_2
commands.description: Measures the S22 REFLECT class of the TRL*/LRM* 2-port calibration.

commands.name: trl_thru
commands.description: Measures the THRU class of the TRL*/LRM* 2-port calibration.

commands.name: sliding_load
commands.description: Specifies a standard as a sliding load during a cal-kit standard definition.

commands.name: sliding_load_set
commands.description: Indicates the sliding load is set and ready to measure.

commands.name: sliding_load_done
commands.description: Indicates the sliding load measurement is done.

commands.name: measure_standard
commands.description: Measures the calibration standard A-G in the current standard class.

commands.name: class_done
commands.description: Completes the measurement of the selected standard class for a calibration type.

commands.name: modify_cal_kit
commands.description: Begins the modify-calibration-kit sequence.

commands.name: kit_done
commands.description: Ends the calibration-kit modification process.

commands.name: define_standard
commands.description: Defines the number of the calibration standard to be modified.

commands.name: standard_done
commands.description: Completes the current standard definition.

commands.name: std_type_open
commands.description: Defines the standard type as an OPEN.

commands.name: std_type_short
commands.description: Defines the standard type as a SHORT.

commands.name: std_type_load
commands.description: Defines the standard type as a LOAD (termination).

commands.name: std_type_delay_thru
commands.description: Defines the standard type as a transmission line (delay/thru) of specified length.

commands.name: std_type_arbitrary
commands.description: Defines the standard type as an arbitrary impedance load.

commands.name: terminal_impedance
commands.description: Specifies the (arbitrary) impedance of the standard.

commands.name: fixed_load
commands.description: Specifies a load standard as fixed (rather than sliding or offset) when defining the standard.

commands.name: offset_load
commands.description: Specifies a standard as an offset load during a standard definition.

commands.name: offset_delay
commands.description: Specifies the one-way electrical delay from the reference plane to the standard.

commands.name: offset_loss
commands.description: Specifies loss (skin effect) along a one-way length of transmission-line offset for a standard.

commands.name: offset_z0
commands.description: Specifies the characteristic impedance of a standard offset.

commands.name: max_frequency
commands.description: Sets the maximum valid frequency of a standard being defined.

commands.name: min_frequency
commands.description: Sets the minimum valid frequency of a standard being defined.

commands.name: coax_offset
commands.description: Selects coaxial offsets (see WAVE) while defining a standard.

commands.name: waveguide_offset
commands.description: Selects waveguide offsets (see COAX) while defining a standard.

commands.name: capacitance_c0
commands.description: Enters the constant term (C0) of the open-circuit capacitance model.

commands.name: capacitance_c1
commands.description: Enters the C1 term of the open-circuit capacitance model.

commands.name: capacitance_c2
commands.description: Enters the C2 term of the open-circuit capacitance model.

commands.name: capacitance_c3
commands.description: Enters the C3 term of the open-circuit capacitance model.

commands.name: class_done_specified
commands.description: Completes specifying the class.

commands.name: label_kit
commands.description: Defines the calibration-kit label.

commands.name: label_standard
commands.description: Defines the calibration-standard label.

commands.name: class_s11
commands.description: Selects the port 1 (S11) one-port standard class A/B/C (built-in 3.5mm kit).

commands.name: class_s22
commands.description: Selects the port 2 (S22) one-port standard class A/B/C (built-in 3.5mm kit).

commands.name: specify_fwd_match
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the forward match (THRU) class.

commands.name: specify_fwd_trans
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the forward transmission (THRU) class.

commands.name: specify_rev_match
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the reverse match (THRU) class.

commands.name: specify_rev_trans
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the reverse transmission (THRU) class.

commands.name: specify_resp_isol
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the response and isolation class.

commands.name: specify_response
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the response calibration class.

commands.name: specify_s11a
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the S11A class.

commands.name: specify_s11b
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the S11B class.

commands.name: specify_s11c
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the S11C class.

commands.name: specify_s22a
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the S22A class.

commands.name: specify_s22b
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the S22B class.

commands.name: specify_s22c
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the S22C class.

commands.name: specify_trl_line
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the TRL* LINE/MATCH class.

commands.name: specify_trl_reflect
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the TRL* REFLECT class.

commands.name: specify_trl_thru
commands.description: Enters the standard numbers for the TRL* THRU class.

commands.name: label_class_fwd
commands.description: Defines the label for the forward match (M) or forward transmission (T) class.

commands.name: label_class_rev
commands.description: Defines the label for the reverse match (M) or reverse transmission (T) class.

commands.name: label_class_resp
commands.description: Defines the label for the response and isolation (I) or response (P) class.

commands.name: label_class_s11
commands.description: Defines the label for the S11A/S11B/S11C class.

commands.name: label_class_s22
commands.description: Defines the label for the S22A/S22B/S22C class.

commands.name: label_class_trl
commands.description: Defines the label for the TRL LINE (L), REFLECT (R), or THRU (T) class.

commands.name: transform
commands.description: Turns the time domain transform on or off (Option 010).

commands.name: bandpass_mode
commands.description: Selects bandpass mode for time domain transforms (Option 010).

commands.name: lowpass_impulse
commands.description: Selects low pass impulse transform (Option 010).

commands.name: lowpass_step
commands.description: Selects low pass step transform (Option 010).

commands.name: set_freq_lowpass
commands.description: Sets the frequency range to harmonically related points required for Low Pass mode (Option 010).

commands.name: demodulation
commands.description: Selects transform demodulation: amplitude, phase, or off (CW time transform; Option 010).

commands.name: gate
commands.description: Turns the time domain gate on or off (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_center
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate center time (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_span
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate span time (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_start
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate start time (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_stop
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate stop time (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_shape_maximum
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate shape to maximum (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_shape_wide
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate shape to wide (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_shape_normal
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate shape to normal (Option 010).

commands.name: gate_shape_minimum
commands.description: Sets the time domain gate shape to minimum (Option 010).

commands.name: window_maximum
commands.description: Sets the time domain window shape to maximum (Option 010).

commands.name: window_normal
commands.description: Sets the time domain window shape to normal (Option 010).

commands.name: window_minimum
commands.description: Sets the time domain window shape to minimum (Option 010).

commands.name: window_value
commands.description: Sets the time domain window shape pulse-width value (Option 010).

commands.name: window_use_memory
commands.description: Uses the current trace memory as the time domain window shape (Option 010).

commands.name: limit_test
commands.description: Sets limit testing on or off.

commands.name: limit_line
commands.description: Sets limit lines on or off.

commands.name: edit_limit_line
commands.description: Begins editing the limit line table.

commands.name: limit_edit_done
commands.description: Completes editing the limit table.

commands.name: limit_stimulus
commands.description: Sets the starting stimulus value of a limit-testing segment.

commands.name: limit_upper
commands.description: Sets the upper limit value for a limit-testing segment.

commands.name: limit_lower
commands.description: Sets the lower limit value for a limit-testing segment.

commands.name: limit_delta
commands.description: Sets the limits delta value from the specified middle value.

commands.name: limit_middle
commands.description: Sets the middle value of delta limits.

commands.name: limit_type
commands.description: Specifies the limit type as flat line (FL), sloping line (SL), or single point (SP).

commands.name: limit_amplitude_offset
commands.description: Sets an amplitude offset value for limit testing.

commands.name: limit_stimulus_offset
commands.description: Sets a stimulus offset value for limit testing.

commands.name: marker_to_amplitude_offset
commands.description: Sets the active marker value as the amplitude offset for limit testing.

commands.name: edit_list
commands.description: Begins editing the frequency list.

commands.name: edit_done
commands.description: Completes editing the frequency list, limit table, or power-meter sensor cal-factor table.

commands.name: clear_list
commands.description: Clears the current frequency list.

commands.name: segment_add
commands.description: Adds a new segment to the list frequency, limit line, or power sensor table.

commands.name: segment_delete
commands.description: Deletes a segment from the list frequency, limit line, or power sensor table.

commands.name: segment_done
commands.description: Completes editing a segment of the list frequency, limit line, or power sensor table.

commands.name: segment_edit
commands.description: Selects a segment of the list frequency, limit line, or power sensor table to be modified.

commands.name: save_register
commands.description: Saves the current instrument state in internal register 1-5.

commands.name: recall_register
commands.description: Recalls internal register 1-5 (RECA5 recalls the USER PRESET).

commands.name: clear_register
commands.description: Clears save/recall register 1-5.

commands.name: clear_all_registers
commands.description: Clears all five save/recall registers.

commands.name: title_register
commands.description: Titles internal register 1-5 (used with SAVE and RECALL).

parameters.name: string
parameters.range: up to 8 alphanumeric chars, first alphabetic

commands.name: title_file
commands.description: Titles the disk file at position 1-5 for subsequent disk access (used with LOAD/STORE/PURG).

parameters.name: string
parameters.range: up to 8 alphanumeric chars, first alphabetic

commands.name: store_to_disk
commands.description: Stores the file at position 1-5 to disk (requires pass control).

commands.name: load_from_disk
commands.description: Loads the file at position 1-5 from disk (requires pass control).

commands.name: purge_file
commands.description: Removes the file at position 1-5 from disk (requires pass control).

commands.name: read_file_titles
commands.description: Reads file titles from disk (requires pass control).

commands.name: copy_from_file_titles
commands.description: Copies disk file titles into the register titles.

commands.name: copy_from_reg_titles
commands.description: Copies register titles into the disk file titles.

commands.name: initialize_disk
commands.description: Initializes the disk (LIF format).

commands.name: store_binary
commands.description: Stores appropriate files to disk as binary files.

commands.name: store_ascii
commands.description: Stores appropriate files to disk as ASCII (CITIfile) files.

commands.name: store_data_array
commands.description: Enables/disables storage of error-corrected data when a file is stored to disk.

commands.name: store_raw_array
commands.description: Enables/disables storage of raw data array(s) when a file is stored to disk.

commands.name: store_format_array
commands.description: Enables/disables storage of formatted data when a file is stored to disk.

commands.name: store_graphics
commands.description: Enables/disables storage of user graphics when a file is stored to disk.

commands.name: print_all
commands.description: Copies the measurement display to the printer.

commands.name: print_color
commands.description: Selects color printing.

commands.name: print_standard
commands.description: Sets the print command to the default selection.

commands.name: plot
commands.description: Plots the display to a graphics plotter.

commands.name: plot_speed
commands.description: Sets the plotting speed to fast or slow.

commands.name: full_page
commands.description: Selects the full-page plot.

commands.name: scale_plot
commands.description: Selects normal full-size plot scale (FULL) or graticule expanded to P1/P2 (GRAT).

commands.name: quadrant_left_upper
commands.description: Sets the plot quadrant to left upper.

commands.name: quadrant_left_lower
commands.description: Sets the plot quadrant to left lower.

commands.name: quadrant_right_upper
commands.description: Sets the plot quadrant to right upper.

commands.name: quadrant_right_lower
commands.description: Sets the plot quadrant to right lower.

commands.name: define_plot_select
commands.description: Selects whether data, memory, graticule, marker(s), and/or text are plotted with PLOT.

commands.name: plot_pen
commands.description: Selects the pen number for data, memory, graticule, marker(s), or text when plotting.

commands.name: line_type
commands.description: Selects the line type of a trace (data or memory) for plotting.

commands.name: plot_grat
commands.description: Selects plotting of the graticule on or off.

commands.name: softkey_labels
commands.description: Selects the plot/print softkey-labels option on or off (applies to OUTPPLOT/OUTPPRIN).

commands.name: default_plot_setup
commands.description: Returns the plotting parameters to the default values.

commands.name: operating_parameters
commands.description: Lists the key parameters for both channels on the display.

commands.name: list_values
commands.description: Displays a tabular listing of all stimulus values and their current measured values.

commands.name: next_page
commands.description: Displays the next page of a tabular listing on the display.

commands.name: restore_display
commands.description: Turns off a tabular listing (OPEP/LISV) and restores the measurement display.

commands.name: menu_on
commands.description: Enables display of a particular menu (must precede MENU{...}).

commands.name: menu_off
commands.description: Turns off the display of the current menu.

commands.name: menu_select
commands.description: Displays the top-level menu for the specified hardkey (must be preceded by MENUON).

commands.name: input_data
commands.description: Inputs error-corrected data; the analyzer stops sweeping and displays the data.

commands.name: input_formatted
commands.description: Inputs formatted data; the analyzer stops sweeping and displays the data.

commands.name: input_raw
commands.description: Inputs raw data (arrays 1-4); the analyzer stops sweeping and displays the data.

commands.name: input_cal_coeff
commands.description: Stores a measurement calibration error-coefficient set (01-12) into instrument memory (see Chapter 5).

commands.name: input_cal_kit
commands.description: Inputs a calibration-kit definition into the USER CAL KIT (FORM1, 714 bytes); save with SAVEUSEK.

commands.name: input_learn_string
commands.description: Inputs a learn string previously obtained with OUTPLEAS (also LRN).

commands.name: input_pm_cal
commands.description: Inputs a power meter calibration array (1 or 2) previously obtained with OUTPPMCAL.

commands.name: output_active
commands.description: Outputs the active entry function value (or the last active function if the entry area is off).

commands.name: output_aperture
commands.description: Outputs the smoothing aperture in stimulus units rather than as a percentage.

commands.name: output_cal_coeff
commands.description: Outputs the active calibration set array (01-12) of the active channel (real, imaginary).

commands.name: output_cal_kit
commands.description: Outputs the active calibration kit (FORM1, 714 bytes of binary data).

commands.name: output_data
commands.description: Outputs the error-corrected data (real, imaginary).

commands.name: output_formatted
commands.description: Outputs the formatted trace data (format depends on the current display format).

commands.name: output_memory
commands.description: Outputs the memory data from the active channel (real, imaginary).

commands.name: output_raw
commands.description: Outputs the uncorrected (raw) data arrays 1-4 for the active channel (real, imaginary).

commands.name: output_marker
commands.description: Outputs the active marker values (marker value, aux value, stimulus).

commands.name: output_marker_stats
commands.description: Outputs the marker statistics (mean, standard deviation, peak-to-peak).

commands.name: output_bandwidth
commands.description: Outputs the results of the bandwidth search (bandwidth, center, Q).

commands.name: output_title
commands.description: Outputs the display title for the active channel (less than 54 characters).

commands.name: output_key
commands.description: Outputs the key code of the last key pressed (63 = invalid key, -1 = knob turn).

commands.name: output_learn_string
commands.description: Outputs the learn string containing the current instrument state.

commands.name: output_error
commands.description: Outputs the next error message from the error queue (error number, string).

commands.name: output_plot_string
commands.description: Outputs the plot string (may go to a plotter or be read by the computer).

commands.name: output_print_string
commands.description: Outputs the print string (may go to a printer or be read by the computer).

commands.name: output_pm_cal
commands.description: Outputs a power meter calibration array (1 or 2).

commands.name: output_limit_pass_fail
commands.description: Outputs the limit-test result for each point (stimulus, result, upper, lower; always Form 4 ASCII).

commands.name: output_limit_fail
commands.description: Outputs the limit-test results only for failed points (stimulus, result, upper, lower; Form 4).

commands.name: output_limit_marker
commands.description: Outputs the limit-test result for the marker position (stimulus, result, upper, lower).

commands.name: output_key_or_knob
commands.description: Outputs a two-byte key code or knob count (positive = key code, negative = encoded knob count).

commands.name: format_internal
commands.description: Sets data transfer format to the analyzer's internal binary format.

commands.name: format_ieee_32
commands.description: Sets data transfer format to IEEE 32-bit floating point.

commands.name: format_ieee_64
commands.description: Sets data transfer format to IEEE 64-bit floating point.

commands.name: format_ascii
commands.description: Sets data transfer format to ASCII.

commands.name: format_ieee_32_lsb
commands.description: Sets data transfer format to IEEE 32-bit floating point with least-significant byte first (PC-DOS compatible).

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clears the status byte, the event status register, and event status register B.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Enables specific bits of the event status register.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Returns the event status register value.

commands.name: event_status_enable_b
commands.description: Enables specific bits of event status register B.

commands.name: event_status_register_b
commands.description: Returns the event status register B value.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Service request enable; the value masks which status-byte bits generate an SRQ.

commands.name: status_byte
commands.description: Reads the status byte.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Reports completion of the next command by setting bit 0 in the event status register (or replying to OPC?).

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Executes an internal self-test and returns the result (0 = pass, 1 = fail).

commands.name: preset
commands.description: Sets the analyzer to the factory preset condition. Equivalent to *RST.

commands.name: common_clear_status
commands.description: Clears the status byte, the event status register, and event status register B.

commands.name: common_event_status_enable
commands.description: Enables specific bits of the standard event status register.

commands.name: common_event_status_register
commands.description: Returns the contents of the standard event status register.

commands.name: common_identify
commands.description: Outputs the identification string "HEWLETT PACKARD, 87aam,0,X.XX" (model and firmware revision).

commands.name: common_operation_complete
commands.description: Sets bit 0 (operation complete) in the event status register when all pending operations complete; *OPC? outputs 1 on completion.

commands.name: common_pass_control_back
commands.description: Sets the HP-IB address the analyzer uses to communicate with an external controller.

commands.name: common_reset
commands.description: Sets the analyzer to the factory preset condition.

commands.name: common_service_request_enable
commands.description: Service request enable; the value masks which status-byte bits generate an SRQ.

commands.name: common_status_byte
commands.description: Reads the status byte.

commands.name: common_self_test
commands.description: Executes an internal self-test and returns the result (0 = pass, 1 = fail).
